comments.body: Claiming a narrow docs-routing sync from current main. Scope: add a dedicated central proof-gap index row for the ABF26 T2.18 UM subspace-design half. This will not prove the UM theorem or close the subspace-design residual.

Docs-routing follow-up pushed to `main` in `6ff921ecf` (`docs: route ABF proximity gaps`).

Added central proof-gap index row: ABF26 T2.18 UM subspace-design half -> #92.

Validation:
- `git diff --check -- docs/kb/audits/ARKLIB-PROOF-GAP-INDEX-2026-06-06.md`
- batch anchor scan for #92/#93/#94/#95/#96/#99/#100/#101/#102/#103/#104/#106 rows

Leaving this open: the UM subspace-design proof remains unsolved; this only adds central ownership/routing.

Claiming + landing the UM (univariate-multiplicity) **D_ux/multiplicity kernel bridge + degree-budget spine** brick (the genuine `D_ux` content of the UM half).

Scope claimed: the symbol-level kernel characterization of `ReedSolomon.Multiplicity.umEvalOnPoints` and the UM degree-budget spine, in my own scratch file `ArkLib/ToMathlib/UMSubspaceDesignProof.lean`. Not yet claiming the full encoder-isomorphism transport / `IsSubspaceDesign umCode` assembly.

Landed in `2b2ca9725` (axiom-clean, `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, no `sorry`):

- `mem_ker_umProj_iff` — `umEvalOnPoints domain s p in ker(proj i)` iff all first `s` formal derivatives `D_ux^j p` vanish at `domain i` (the `D_ux` operation made explicit: the `s` UM symbol components ARE the `s` derivative evaluations).
- `iterate_derivative_eval_eq_zero_iff_le_rootMultiplicity` — the **D_ux <-> rootMultiplicity bridge**: over a field with `(s-1)!` invertible (the ABF26 A.7 characteristic condition `char F >= k >= s`), the first `s` derivatives of a nonzero `p` vanish at `t` iff `s <= rootMultiplicity t p`. Via Mathlib `lt_rootMultiplicity_iff_isRoot_iterate_derivative_of_mem_nonZeroDivisors`.
- `mem_ker_umProj_iff_le_rootMultiplicity` — combined: UM symbol vanishes at coord `i` iff `domain i` is a root of multiplicity `>= s`.
- `um_card_mult_ge_mul_le` — the **UM degree-budget spine** `s * #{i : mult >= s} <= k-1`, the multiplicity-code counterpart of the GK16 folded-Wronskian spine `sum_rootMultiplicity_foldedWronskian_le`, proved directly from `Polynomial.sum_rootMultiplicity_le_natDegree`.

This replaces the folded-Wronskian machinery of the GK16 half with genuine `D_ux`/Hasse-multiplicity content. Remaining for the full UM `IsSubspaceDesign`: the encoder-isomorphism transport (realize `A <= umCode` as a degree-`<k` polynomial family, mirroring `frs_degreeBudget_of_finrank_le`) and the rate-arithmetic wrapper. Continuing.

Landed the next two UM bricks (both axiom-clean, `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, no `sorry`), in `ArkLib/ToMathlib/UMSubspaceDesignProof.lean`:

**Brick 2 — UM residual + rate-arithmetic** (`38dea1554`):
- `CodingTheory.UMDegreeBudget` — the UM degree-budget residual `sum_i dim A_i <= (dim A)(k-1)`, the multiplicity-code counterpart of `GK16DegreeBudget`.
- `CodingTheory.um_is_subspaceDesign_of_budget` — **ABF26 T2.18 UM half**, reduced to `UMDegreeBudget`: proves `IsSubspaceDesign (umCode domain k s)` for the repaired profile `tau(r) = (k-1)/n` on `[s]` (and `1` off `[s]`), fully and unconditionally on the rate arithmetic, in exact parallel with the FRS `frs_is_subspaceDesign_gk16`. Connects to the general L2.17 lower bound `subspaceDesign_tau_lower` without weakening the theorem surface.

**Brick 3 — rank-1 UMDegreeBudget discharge** (now in `main`):
- `CodingTheory.um_degreeBudget_rank_one` — for a single nonzero degree-`<k` polynomial `p`, the UM codeword `c = umEvalOnPoints domain s p` satisfies `sum_i dim(span{c} cap ker(eval_i)) <= k-1 = dim(span{c})*(k-1)`, **unconditionally** given the A.7 characteristic condition `(s-1)!` invertible. Each per-coordinate rank is 0/1; a zero symbol is a root of multiplicity `>= s` (via `mem_ker_umProj_iff_le_rootMultiplicity`), and the spine `um_card_mult_ge_mul_le` bounds the count. This is exactly the rank-1 instance consumed by ABF26 Lemma 2.17.

**State.** The UM half is now formalized down to a single named residual `UMDegreeBudget`, exactly mirroring the GK16 half`s `GK16DegreeBudget`, with the rank-1 case discharged unconditionally. The genuine `D_ux`/Hasse-multiplicity content (kernel <-> rootMultiplicity bridge + degree-budget spine) is fully proven.

**Remaining (leaving #92 open).** The general (arbitrary `dim A`) `UMDegreeBudget` discharge needs the encoder-isomorphism transport (realize `A <= umCode` as an independent degree-`<k` polynomial family) plus a Wronskian-of-derivatives multiplicity argument, mirroring the GK16 structural residual `GK16Claim16StructuralData` / `frs_degreeBudget_of_finrank_le`. That brick is open for whoever picks it up next.
I am claiming this issue and working on it.
